rationale: The citing paper uses this reference in several functional ways, not just as a passing citation. It cites Van Heerde, Gijsbrechts, and Pauwels (2008) to motivate the Dutch competitive context, to position prior loyalty work that operationalizes loyalty as state dependence, and to justify a similar price-index construction in its own data. These uses shape framing and empirical implementation of the store-choice model. The citing paper does not directly challenge or formally extend the cited paper's price-war theory, so the relation is best characterized as applying prior ideas/procedures.
relation_description: The citing paper applies elements from Van Heerde, Gijsbrechts, and Pauwels (2008) in its own study design, especially the state-dependence view of loyalty and a similar chain-level price-index measurement approach. It also uses the cited paper's findings as contextual evidence for competitive pressure in the Dutch grocery market.
